_comment: PSA Module Import Template - Use this as a guide for creating module import JSON files
_module_code_note: Must start with 'MODULE_' and contain only uppercase letters, numbers, and underscores. Example: MODULE_EV_CHARGING, MODULE_EV_PARKING
title: Example Module Title
_title_note: Human-readable module name. This will be displayed in the UI.
description: Optional description of what this module covers and when it should be used.
_description_note: Optional field. Provide context about the module's purpose and scope.
_import_source_note: Optional. Name of the source file or system that generated this import.
_mode_note: Either 'REPLACE' (default) or 'APPEND'. REPLACE deletes all existing module content before importing. APPEND adds to existing content.

module_questions._comment: Module questions are additive, technology/situation-specific questions. They must NOT reference baseline questions.
_validation_rules: Question IDs must start with 'MODULEQ_' followed by module code (e.g., MODULEQ_EV_CHARGING_001)
Question text must be non-generic and technology/situation dependent
Each question must be assigned to a valid discipline/subtype pair
Discipline and subtype must match semantic content of the question
Event trigger must match the question's semantic content
No baseline question IDs (BASE-*) are allowed
module_questions._id_note: Must be unique and follow pattern: MODULEQ_<MODULE_CODE>_###
module_questions.text: Is physical access to [specific asset/location] restricted to authorized personnel?
module_questions._text_note: Must be specific, non-generic, and technology/situation dependent. Avoid phrases like 'supports physical security'.
module_questions._order_note: Display order (1-based). Questions will be shown in this order.
module_questions._discipline_id_note: UUID of the discipline from public.disciplines table. Use scripts/get_discipline_uuids_for_modules.js to get valid UUIDs.

_discipline_examples.ACS: Access Control Systems - for access, restricted areas, authorized personnel questions
_discipline_examples.VSS: Video Surveillance Systems - for camera, video coverage, surveillance questions
_discipline_examples.EMR: Emergency Management & Resilience - for panic, duress, assistance, emergency questions
_discipline_examples.SMG: Security Management & Governance - for procedures, responsibilities, coordination questions

module_questions._discipline_subtype_id_note: UUID of the subtype from public.discipline_subtypes table. Must belong to the discipline_id. Generic subtypes (General, Other) are rejected.
module_questions._asset_or_location_note: Required. Must be non-empty. Describes what asset or location the question applies to (e.g., 'EV charging equipment', 'Parking garage').
module_questions._event_trigger_note: One of: FIRE, TAMPERING, IMPACT, OUTAGE, OTHER

_event_trigger_rules.TAMPERING: Required for: access, inspection, cable, hardware, lighting/visibility questions
_event_trigger_rules.OTHER: Required for: panic, duress, assistance, emergency questions
_event_trigger_rules.OUTAGE: Required for: recovery, restoration, continuity, coordination questions

module_questions.text: Is video coverage implemented for [specific location] to support incident detection?

module_ofcs._comment: Module OFCs (Options For Consideration) are additive, module-specific recommendations.
_validation_rules: OFC IDs must start with 'MOD_OFC_' followed by module code (e.g., MOD_OFC_EV_CHARGING_001)
OFC text must be PSA-scope (physical security only, no cyber controls)
Sources are optional but recommended for traceability
module_ofcs._ofc_id_note: Must be unique and follow pattern: MOD_OFC_<MODULE_CODE>_###
module_ofcs.ofc_text: Consider implementing [specific physical security measure] at [location] to [achieve physical security outcome].
module_ofcs._ofc_text_note: Must be physical security focused. Cyber controls (encryption, 2FA, authentication) are NOT allowed in OFCs.
module_ofcs._order_index_note: Display order (1-based). OFCs will be shown in this order.
module_ofcs._source_system_note: Optional. Identifier for the source system (e.g., 'VULN_JSON', 'IST_HTML'). Used for traceability.
module_ofcs._source_ofc_id_note: Optional. Original OFC ID from source system. Stored for traceability but NOT displayed.
module_ofcs._source_ofc_num_note: Optional. Original OFC number from source system.
module_ofcs._sources_note: Optional array of source references. URL can be empty if label is provided (for reference-only sources).

module_ofcs.ofc_text: An option for consideration is [specific measure] to [achieve outcome].

risk_drivers._comment: Risk drivers provide context about cyber/fraud vulnerabilities that may impact physical security.
_validation_rules: Risk drivers are context-only (never become questions or OFCs)
One driver per vulnerability per type (CYBER_DRIVER or FRAUD_DRIVER)
Driver text must describe a single initiating cause with physical-security impact
No garbage concatenation (lists of options, repeated phrases)
risk_drivers.driver_text: Unauthorized access to [system] may enable physical tampering or unsafe conditions at [location].
risk_drivers._driver_text_note: Must describe a single initiating cause with physical-security impact. Should be a complete sentence, not a list of options.

source_locator._comment: Optional. Used to track which vulnerability this driver came from.

risk_drivers.driver_text: Payment skimming or data theft at [location] may increase criminal targeting of [location] and associated users.
